forecaster_note: Surfline — Peter Aquilina, Lead Forecaster: "Clean morning with light to moderate offshores. Stronger SW'lies through the arvo. Buoys showing just small leftovers combine and with light clean NW-WNW winds we are in for clean and mellow waves. Most beaches to struggle for size with only the most exposed swell magnets offering occasional chest high waves. Surf a very high volume board and get in early if you do surf. A brisk SW wind will probably reduce quality a touch on the exposures." — Surf Guru read: the ENE pulse has drained to 3.5ft @ 10s from 67°, only ~120 kJ at 9am, so it's clean but genuinely weak. Long Reef and Curl Curl are the only two holding waist-to-chest. The real story is Friday and the weekend: a big dead-S groundswell (6.8–7.1ft @ 11s from 181–186°) arrives, peaking Sunday at 1110 kJ.

wind.summary: Light WNW offshore first up (9–12 kph, 285°) holding clean through mid-morning, then it swings SW and gets up — 19 kph gusting 29 by 3pm and 24 gusting 39 by dusk. Real window is dawn to about noon.

shark.summary: ⚠️ Shark observed at North Curl Curl Beach 8:23am Wed 22 Jul — water evacuated (SLSNSW via DPI Fisheries). No detections logged at Dee Why, Long Reef or Manly in the last 24h.

days.label: Today
days.note: Clean but weak — the ENE leftovers are down to 3.5ft @ 10s from 67° and only ~120 kJ, roughly half of yesterday. Light WNW offshore keeps it groomed until about noon, then a brisk SW gets up (19 kph gusting 29 by 3pm). Long Reef and Curl Curl are the only two holding waist-to-chest; Dee Why and Manly are thigh-to-stomach. High-volume board, early session, low expectations. ⚠️ Shark evacuation at North Curl Curl yesterday morning.

dee-why.window: WNW offshore is clean but the swell is thin — 120 kJ won't push much through the bank. Low 8:40am then filling to the 3:44pm high; the incoming push mid-morning is the only real shot. SW gets up after noon and it's done.

tip.label: Frontside tip
tip.text: 67° is still your good angle, just without the energy behind it. At 120 kJ the Point won't throw for you — paddle hard, get to your feet early and generate everything off the bottom turn. Honestly, this is a Sub Zero / high-volume day, not a performance day.

long-reef.window: The swell magnet Aquilina is talking about — takes what little ENE there is and turns it into waist-to-shoulder. Best of the four today. Get there before 10am while the WNW still holds.

tip.label: Frontside tip
tip.text: The reef doubles what the beachies get on a weak day. Sit deeper than feels comfortable and take the first of each set — the wave count will be low, so don't burn the good ones waiting.

alerts: 🦈 Shark observed at North Curl Curl 8:23am yesterday (22 Jul) — water was evacuated. Check the SharkSmart map before you paddle out.
curl-curl.window: Second-best size behind Long Reef and the banks are still working, but there was a shark evacuation here yesterday morning. If you go, surf the southern end with people around.

tip.label: Backside tip
tip.text: Weak ENE means the lefts will be soft and fat. On your backhand at 120 kJ you'll need to stay high and pump rather than hunt a pocket that isn't there.

manly.window: Smallest of the four and the least worth the drive today. Clean under the WNW but thigh-to-stomach at best.

tip.label: Backside tip
tip.text: Only worth it if you're already in town. Walk the stretch and find the one bank with sand on it — everything else will be closing out on the shallows.

days.label: Tomorrow
days.note: Everything changes. The new S groundswell arrives and energy jumps 4× to ~500 kJ, with Dee Why up to chest-to-head and Long Reef head to 2ft overhead. Wind is offshore SW all day but gusty first up (16 gusting 35 kph at 6am) before easing right off by midday — 11am–1pm is the clean window. Consistency drops to 70%, so expect waits between sets.

dee-why.window: Best-rated of the four on Friday. Gusty SW early smooths right out by midday — 11am–1pm is the pick. Chest-to-head with real weight behind it for the first time this week.

long-reef.window: Biggest of the four — head to 2ft overhead as the S swell fills in. Surfline marks it down for the early SW gusts; wait for the midday lull if you want it clean.

curl-curl.window: Solid chest-to-overhead peaks. Cross-offshore at 9am turning cleaner by noon — good size, slightly less groomed than Dee Why.

manly.window: The southern headland starts shadowing as the swell swings S — smallest of the four again. Fine if you want the manageable option.

days.label: Weekend
days.note: The big one. A dead-S groundswell peaks at 1110 kJ with 100% consistency — 6.8ft @ 11s from 186° Saturday and 7.1ft @ 11s from 181° Sunday. That angle is the problem: near-dead south wraps poorly into Dee Why and is heavily shadowed at Manly. Long Reef and Curl Curl are the S-exposed picks, both up to overhead-plus. Wind is offshore both mornings — Sat WNW 12–16 kph, Sun WSW easing to near-glassy by noon. Sunday midday is the cleanest window of the weekend.

alerts: Near-dead-S swell (181–186°) — 1110 kJ on paper but it wraps badly into Dee Why. Expect well under what the energy number suggests; the Point is the only part that grooms it.
dee-why.window: Both mornings run offshore. The Point is the play — a S swell wraps down and can hold a long clean right while the beachies stay shadowed. Rates fair-good both days despite the modest size, which tells you it'll be well-shaped rather than big.

alerts: Peaks at 2x overhead — step-up board and treat it with respect.
💨 Sat 9am WNW 16 gusting 25 kph — offshore but enough to put chop on the bigger faces.
long-reef.window: Most S-exposed and the size standout of the weekend — overhead to double overhead. Saturday 8–11am rates best; Sunday midday goes near-glassy (3 kph) but is the biggest. This is the serious session of the week.

curl-curl.window: Takes the S swell far better than Dee Why — solid overhead peaks with the offshore holding them open. Best size-plus-shape balance of the weekend, and more manageable than Long Reef. Saturday morning is the pick.

alerts: S swell is heavily shadowed by the southern headland — smallest of the four by a wide margin despite the 1110 kJ figure.
manly.window: Clean but shadowed. Only worth it if the bigger spots are too much, or you want an easy paddle.
